demographics.living_situation: lives at home with a live-in carer; bedbound at baseline

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't catch my breath no matter what I do, and my chest feels completely heavy and exhausted. (Carer speaking for him: he's really struggling to breathe and it's got much worse since this morning.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: His breathing has got dramatically worse over the last half a day and nothing we try makes any difference — sitting him up hasn't helped at all.

symptom_narrative.onset: started getting short of breath after a bad choking episode about a day and a half ago, and it has ramped up fast over roughly the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: worsening for about 12 hours, much worse today
symptom_narrative.character: a desperate air-hunger feeling — like he can't get enough air in — with a dry, ineffective cough
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it has ever been; he's exhausted from the effort of breathing and can barely get words out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his chest feels heavy and tired all over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting steadily worse, no let-up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — sitting upright makes no difference at all
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps worsening on its own regardless of position
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feels hot/feverish, very tired and a bit muddled or drowsy; the carer says his lips look dusky
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has never had breathing trouble like this before and has no known heart problem
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely bedbound now, too breathless to move or talk much

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a bad choking / food-going-down-the-wrong-way episode a day and a half ago
no known heart problem — never been told he has heart failure

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: just finished a course of antibiotic tablets given after the choking episode

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: bedbound at baseline and cared for at home by a live-in carer who is present and made contact because she cannot manage his breathing distress

objective_data_offered.timing: just now, on a home fingertip pulse oximeter, in a well-ventilated room
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home fingertip oximeter; low readings can be affected by cold or poorly perfused fingers, but a reading this low with visible distress is concerning

objective_data_offered.timing: displayed on the same home pulse oximeter
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: pulse-rate figure from a consumer oximeter

objective_data_offered.timing: taken by the carer with a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: carer reports his lips and around his mouth look bluish/dusky, he is breathing very fast and hard using a lot of effort, and he seems drowsy and slow to respond
he is in a well-ventilated room and the low oxygen reading is not improving despite resting
